title: holographis haenkeana
intro: Holographis haenkeana, also known as Hawaiian plantain or ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant species endemic to Hawaii, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and the treatment of skin conditions. The health benefits of Holographis haenkeana include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and topical applications, making it a valuable resource in traditional Hawaiian medicine.
